ocr.text: In the pursuit of understanding the effectiveness of our training programs, a comprehensive analysis was conducted involving
the demographic profiles of the participants. The data presented in the accompanying table illustrates key insights derived from
interviews conducted with a total of 23 trainees.
Demographics
Total number of trainees interviewed  23
Gender of trainee interviewees
Male  9
Female  14
Total number of global health experiences (GHE)  38
Average number of weeks per trainee spent on GHEs  6.85
Level of training during GHE
Medical student year 1  47%
Medical student year 2  24%
Medical student year 3  8%
Medical student year 4  21%
Continents visited
Africa  53%
Americas  21%
Asia  16%
Other  11%
Trainees who participated in pre-departure training  60%
Trainees who participated Debriefing  36%
Average number of years between most recent GHE and interview  1.47
This sample size provides a robust foundation for assessing the various factors influencing trainee experiences and outcomes.
The first column, labeled "Age Group," offers a breakdown of the trainees into relevant categories, facilitating a nuanced
understanding of how age may correlate with training efficacy and engagement levels. Additionally, the "Education Level"
column highlights the diverse educational backgrounds of the participants, ranging from high school diplomas to advanced
degrees. This diversity is crucial, as it allows for the examination of how prior educational experiences impact the assimilation

of training material and overall satisfaction with the program. 

By carefully analyzing these demographic metrics, we aim to
identify trends and potential areas for improvement within our training initiatives, ensuring they are tailored to meet the varied
needs of our diverse trainee population. The insights gained from this analysis will inform future program enhancements and
contribute to a more effective training environment.
